channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Los Angeles district attorney to update public in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles district attorney will give an update Friday on the case of Lyle and Erik Menendez, brothers who are seeking release after their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge cancels trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams but leaves corruption charges intact for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cancelled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise him on how to handle the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump signals he’s open to multiple budget bills instead of just one
item.description: Hours after the Senate approved a spending framework, President Donald Trump said in a Fox News interview that he’s open to approving the spending plan in multiple bills “as long as we get to the same point.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: SS United States to embark on final voyage to Florida
item.description: A historic ocean liner departed on Thursday for her final voyage, where it heads towards its watery grave off of Florida's coast.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, NewsNation

item.title: Jurors begin deliberating in the trial of a man charged with trying to kill Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Jurors began deliberating Friday in the trial of a New Jersey man charged with stabbing and trying to kill Salman Rushdie on a New York lecture stage. The jury got the case after lawyers delivered closing arguments to wrap up seven days of testimony in Chautauqua County Court.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICE, Homeland Security raid bakery, charge owners with harboring illegal immigrants
item.description: Bakery owners in Texas are facing felony charges after ICE and HSI arrested eight suspected illegal immigrants employed by the shop.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chile 'sending their criminals' to U.S., Orange County DA says
item.description: Orange County District Attorney Todd Spitzer says Chilean crime rings are “continuing to terrorize Americans” and is calling for Chile to be removed from a U.S. visa waiver program.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Senate delivers on Trump's agenda after overnight vote-a-rama and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge allows Trump's mass firings of federal workers to move forward
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge in Washington has allowed President Donald Trump’s mass firings of federal workers to move forward. U.S. District Judge Christopher Cooper decided Thursday he could not grant a motion from unions representing the workers to temporarily block the layoffs.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Luigi Mangione set for first court appearance since his arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare in New York City and leading authorities on a five-day search is scheduled to be in court for the first time since his December arraignment on state murder and terror charges.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell announced Thursday that he won’t seek reelection next year, ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce GOP populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Judge extends temporary block to huge cuts in National Institutes of Health research funding
item.description: A federal judge is continuing to block the Trump administration’s drastic cuts in medical research funding.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at $4B in funding for California high-speed rail
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Trump administration is once again targeting California's controversial high-speed rail project, with federal transportation officials on Thursday announcing an investigation and possible withdrawal of about $4 billion in federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials investigating bomb threat at Kennedy Center, police say
item.description: The Kennedy Center was evacuated on Thursday after a bomb threat was reported at the venue, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: David Boren, a former Oklahoma governor and veteran US senator and university president, dies at 83
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Former Oklahoma Gov. David Boren, who became one of the nation's youngest governors in the 1970s at age 33 and later helped shape national intelligence as a U.S. senator, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: TIM TALLEY and SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Creative control of James Bond franchise given to Amazon MGM
item.description: Amazon MGM Studios and longtime Bond custodians Michael G. Wilson and Barbara Broccoli formed a new joint venture in which they will co-own James Bond intellectual property rights — but Amazon MGM will have creative control.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, Fox 5

item.title: Police arrest man kayaking in Bellagio Fountains during Trump protest
item.description: Because the charges are misdemeanors, a judge released Barnes without him having to post bond, records said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell won't seek reelection to Senate
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) announced Thursday that he is not seeking reelection in 2026, bringing an end to the longest-serving Senate leader’s political career.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Menendez brothers discuss 'bullying and trauma' in prison in rare public remarks, new podcast interview
item.description: Convicted parental killer Erik Menendez discusses the "trauma and bullying" of his life in a California prison in a new interview from behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most worried DOGE cuts could hurt services in their communities: Survey
item.description: Most U.S. adults in the latest survey, 62 percent, disagreed with the notion that the president is authorized to terminate federal employees who do not align with his views or agenda.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Navy veteran's service dogs denied access on plane in Denver
item.description: A Navy veteran missed his flight after not being allowed to take his service dogs on a plane from Denver to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Shaul Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social justice law professor defends anti-Israel protesters who blocked traffic to Chicago airport
item.description: A Northwestern Law School clinic is offering free legal defense to a group of anti-Israel activists who shut down traffic to Chicago's O'Hare International Airport last spring.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month into second term: Survey
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating appears to be underwater as many suggest he may be overstepping his authority, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: IRS plans to lay off 7,000 probationary workers beginning Thursday, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off roughly 7,000 workers in Washington and around the country beginning Thursday, a person familiar with the plans told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This rookie QB card could be worth $50K if you pull it from a new Topps set
item.description: Denver Broncos rookie quarterback Bo Nix impressed on the field, and now a Colorado sports card store is ready to pay $50,000 to keep a unique card featuring him close to home.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KRON4

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: New, cheaper iPhone 16e has 'best battery life ever,' Apple says
item.description: "iPhone 16e has the best battery life ever on a 6.1-inch iPhone, lasting up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE," Apple said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds rally against firing of federal workers, emphasizing threats to public health
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hundreds of current and former federal employees rallied Wednesday outside the Department of Health and Human Services headquarters, protesting recent firings and their effect on public health, and directing much of their ire at Elon Musk and his agency's efforts to cut spending.
item.dc:creator: NATHAN ELLGREN and ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: RFK Jr. eyes childhood vaccine schedule review, says 'nothing off limits' in HHS meeting
item.description: Kennedy urged his new staff to drop any preconceived notions of who he is, but also said he wouldn’t stop asking “difficult questions” that have led to “a lot of unpopular conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order to expand White House control of independent agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aiming to expand the White House’s authority over various independent regulatory agencies, a move likely to prompt legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
